topic: Two colleagues are both considered for the same promotion
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed, a grating soundtrack to my mounting frustration. I watched Sarah across the cubicle farm, her smile bright and effortless as she discussed the upcoming project with Mr. Henderson. Every word she spoke, every confident gesture, felt like a deliberate slight aimed directly at me. My jaw tightened, the muscles in my neck corded with a tension I couldn't shake. I meticulously organized the data for the presentation, my movements sharp and overly precise, a silent declaration of my superior competence, even though I knew it wouldn't matter. When the announcement came, the air seemed to thin. Sarah received the commendation with a genuine, warm nod. I just nodded back, the movement stiff, and felt a cold, heavy weight settle in my stomach, a dull ache that radiated outward.

[story 2]

"Another one for her," I muttered under my breath, the sound barely audible over the keyboard clatter. I leaned back in my chair, deliberately letting my shoulders slump, trying to mimic the relaxed posture I saw on Sarah’s face during the meeting. My hands, however, were clenched into tight fists on the desk. I picked up a pen, turning it over and over until the plastic felt slick beneath my fingers. It was infuriating how effortlessly she seemed to glide through these opportunities, while I felt like I was perpetually stuck in the background, invisible. When the VP finally spoke, the applause seemed to wash over Sarah, a wave of genuine approval. I focused intensely on the screen, my gaze fixed on the text, willing the feeling of inadequacy to simply evaporate.

[story 3]

The coffee in my mug was suddenly too hot, the steam rising in wispy, angry plumes that mirrored the heat building behind my eyes. I kept glancing toward Sarah’s desk, cataloging the subtle ways she interacted with the senior staff—the knowing glances, the easy rapport. A bitter taste coated my tongue. I started typing with unnecessary speed, the keys slamming against the board, each keystroke imbued with a sharp, irritable energy. I felt a distinct prickling sensation across my skin, a restless energy demanding release. When the decision was finalized, I offered a polite, measured response. My voice came out flat, devoid of any warmth, and I immediately turned back to my work, focusing solely on the meticulous details, trying to drown out the sharp, unpleasant feeling churning deep within my chest.
